title: Data Dictionary annotation autosave silently wipes related_tables (and other list/dict fields) on every edit
claim: TableDetail's 1s-debounced autosave payload omits related_tables (and description), but the backend PUT does data.get('related_tables', []) and unconditionally assigns it, so any admin-curated related_tables annotation (consumed by the agent-context retriever) is destroyed the first time a user types in any annotation field. description survives because its .get() default is the existing value; the list/dict fields are clobber-with-default. Partial-update semantics differ per field with no contract on either side.
chain: TableDetail.tsx:157-166 autosave payload (no related_tables) -> dictionarySlice.ts:245-255 PUT -> apps/workspaces/api/views.py:537-538 related_tables = data.get('related_tables', []); tk.related_tables = related_tables -> views.py:556 tk.save() wipes stored value

votes.evidence:
Re-traced the full chain myself:

1. Frontend payload omits related_tables and description. /Users/bderenzi/Code/dimagi/scout/frontend/src/pages/DataDictionaryPage/TableDetail.tsx:160-166 builds the autosave body with exactly five keys: `use_cases`, `data_quality_notes`, `refresh_frequency`, `owner`, `column_notes`. No `related_tables`, no `description`. The effect at lines 191-209 skips the initial render (hasChangedRef guard) but fires `saveAnnotations()` on any subsequent debounced change — i.e. the first time a user types in any annotation field.

2. Slice passes the partial body through verbatim. /Users/bderenzi/Code/dimagi/scout/frontend/src/store/dictionarySlice.ts:252-255: `api.put(... /data-dictionary/tables/${schema}.${table}/, annotations)` — no merge with existing annotation state.

3. Backend clobbers with default. /Users/bderenzi/Code/dimagi/scout/apps/workspaces/api/views.py:520 `related_tables = data.get("related_tables", [])` then :534 `tk.related_tables = related_tables` and :538 `tk.save()` — unconditional assignment of `[]` when the key is absent. By contrast :529 `tk.description = data.get("description", tk.description)` preserves description via the existing-value default, exactly as the finding states. (Finding cited :537-538; actual lines are :520/:534/:538 — immaterial drift.)

4. Consumer and reachability. Route is live: /Users/bderenzi/Code/dimagi/scout/apps/workspaces/api/urls.py:18 wires `data-dictionary/tables/<str:qualified_name>/` to the view with the PUT above; the Data Dictionary page is a shipped UI surface. The destroyed field feeds the agent system prompt: /Users/bderenzi/Code/dimagi/scout/apps/agents/graph/base.py:730-731 (`KnowledgeRetriever(workspace)` → `retriever.retrieve()`) → /Users/bderenzi/Code/dimagi/scout/apps/knowledge/services/retriever.py:95-107 renders `related_tables` (including dict-form join hints) into the "Related Tables" knowledge section.

5. Exposure window. The frontend has no editor for `related_tables` (grep over frontend/src shows only type defs and read-through at dictionarySlice.ts:36,74,150,236); the only writers are Django admin (/Users/bderenzi/Code/dimagi/scout/apps/knowledge/admin.py:26, fieldset "Relationships") or direct API callers that include the key. So loss requires admin-curated data to exist first — consistent with LATENT status — and once a user edits any annotation in the UI, the curated join hints are silently zeroed with no error on either side.
votes.notes: One minor overstatement in the title only: "(and other list/dict fields)" — use_cases/data_quality_notes/column_notes do share the same clobber-with-default backend semantics (views.py:530-531,535-536), but the current frontend payload always includes those keys, so in practice only related_tables is wiped by this UI path. The claim body itself is precise about this. Status (LATENT) and impact (data-loss) are correctly classified; the path is fully reachable today (live route, shipped UI, agent-context consumer).

title: Recipe is_shared / is_public privacy toggles enforce nothing; 'private' recipes/runs visible and runnable by all members; share-creation UI gone but public endpoints live
claim: save_as_recipe tool, model help_text, and the UI 'Share with project' checkbox all promise is_shared=False means only the creator can see/run a recipe, but every recipe/run view filters by workspace only — never by is_shared or created_by — so all recipes and runs are visible, editable, runnable, and deletable by every member; the agent actively makes a false privacy claim. RecipeRun.is_public is settable via PATCH but has no UI; Recipe.is_public/share_token are dead model fields whose save() still mints secrets.token_urlsafe(32). Thread/recipe-run public endpoints, token minting, and public pages remain live after the 2026-06-04 share-UI removal (9783eb2 was UI-only), so pre-removal tokens still grant unauthenticated access to messages + full artifact/recipe code+data with no revocation or audit UI; PublicRecipePage is unrouted and fetches a nonexistent endpoint; uiSlice.updateThreadSharing/setActiveDomainByTenantId are dead with phantom is_public/share_token types the backend never returns.

votes.evidence:
PRIVACY PROMISE vs ENFORCEMENT (re-traced end to end):
1. The promise: apps/agents/tools/recipe_tool.py:86-88 docstring: "is_shared: If True, all workspace members can view and run this recipe. Default is False (only the creator can see it)." Tool is live — wired into the agent graph at apps/agents/graph/base.py:695 (`tools.append(create_recipe_tool(workspace, user))`) and advertised in the system prompt at base.py:682. Model help_text repeats it: apps/recipes/models.py:67-70 ("If true, all project members can view and run this recipe"). UI checkbox "Share with project" exists today: frontend/src/pages/RecipesPage/RecipeDetail.tsx:207-214 and RecipeRunDetail.tsx:207-214.
2. The enforcement gap: every recipe view filters by workspace only. apps/recipes/api/views.py:38 `Recipe.objects.filter(workspace=workspace)` (list); :55 `Recipe.objects.get(pk=recipe_id, workspace=workspace)` (get/put/delete via _get_recipe); :94 (run); :137+140 `RecipeRun.objects.filter(recipe=recipe)` (run list); :154-158 (run PATCH). No view references is_shared or created_by. resolve_workspace_drf (apps/workspaces/workspace_resolver.py:12-32) checks WorkspaceMembership only — any member passes. Routes are live: config/urls.py:63 includes apps/recipes/urls.py under /api/workspaces/<id>/recipes/, and all five views are wired there. So a "private" (is_shared=False) recipe is listable, readable, editable, runnable, and deletable by every workspace member; the agent's privacy claim is false. CONFIRMED.

PUBLIC ENDPOINTS / TOKENS:
3. RecipeRun.is_public settable via PATCH with no UI: RecipeRunUpdateSerializer fields = ["is_shared", "is_public"] (apps/recipes/api/serializers.py:108-113); RecipeRun.save() mints secrets.token_urlsafe(32) when is_public set (models.py:387-392). Commit 9783eb2 (verified via git show: "UI-only removal... No backend endpoints, models, serializers, or public viewer routes/pages were touched") removed only the public checkbox; onUpdateRun signatures still accept is_public (RecipesPage.tsx:125) but no control sends it. Public run endpoint live at config/urls.py:101-105 → PublicRecipeRunView (views.py:169-185, AllowAny, no auth), serving variable_values + step_results. CONFIRMED.
4. Recipe.is_public/share_token dead fields: models.py:71-82 define them; Recipe.save() (models.py:116-121) still mints token_urlsafe(32); but RecipeUpdateSerializer (serializers.py:71-76) exposes only name/description/prompt/variables/is_shared — no API path sets Recipe.is_public. CONFIRMED dead.
5. Thread public path live: config/urls.py:106-110 → public_thread_view (apps/chat/thread_views.py:227-257), unauthenticated, returns messages + full artifact code AND data (_get_thread_artifacts, thread_views.py:52-68). _get_public_thread requires is_shared=True + token (thread_views.py:30-37). Thread.save() (apps/chat/models.py:41-47) mints token when is_shared=True. Note: it is slightly WORSE than "pre-removal tokens still work" — the authenticated PATCH /share/ endpoint (thread_views.py:162-197) is still live, so new public tokens can still be minted today via direct API call; only the UI affordance is gone. No revocation/audit UI exists (only API PATCH is_shared=false).

DEAD FRONTEND CODE:
6. PublicRecipePage: defined at frontend/src/pages/PublicRecipePage.tsx:47; grep shows zero importers; App.tsx routes only /shared/runs/ and /shared/threads/ (App.tsx:21-22). It fetches `/api/recipes/shared/${token}/` (PublicRecipePage.tsx:55) — no such route exists (config/urls.py has only /api/recipes/runs/shared/; apps/recipes/urls.py has no shared path). CONFIRMED.
7. uiSlice.updateThreadSharing (uiSlice.ts:34-38, 76-93): grep finds no callers — dead. Thread/ThreadShareState types declare is_public + share_token (uiSlice.ts:10-12, 19-20), but the threads list endpoint (_list_threads, thread_views.py:79-91) returns neither, and Thread model has no is_public field at all. Minor correction: share_token IS returned by the GET/PATCH /share/ endpoint (thread_views.py:48, 183) — it is "never returned" only for the list endpoint that populates Thread[]; is_public is genuinely never returned anywhere. setActiveDomainByTenantId is dead (definition-only, no-op stub with underscore params) but lives in frontend/src/store/domainSlice.ts:23,73 — NOT uiSlice.ts as the finding's file list implies. CONFIRMED with file-attribution correction.
votes.notes: Verdict is CONFIRMED: every load-bearing claim reproduced independently, and status (BROKEN-NOW) and impact (security) are correct as stated. Two trivia-level corrections: (a) setActiveDomainByTenantId is in domainSlice.ts, not uiSlice.ts; (b) "share_token the backend never returns" is true for the threads-list endpoint feeding the Thread[] type but the dedicated /share/ endpoint does return it (is_public, however, is never returned anywhere — Thread has no such column). One aggravating detail the finding understates: thread public tokens are not merely a pre-removal legacy — the authenticated PATCH /api/workspaces/<id>/threads/<tid>/share/ endpoint is still live and mints fresh public tokens on is_shared=true, so any member can still create unauthenticated share links via direct API today; 9783eb2 removed only the UI. Reachability: all paths live (recipe routes wired in config/urls.py:63 + apps/recipes/urls.py; save_as_recipe registered in agent graph base.py:695; "Share with project" checkboxes render in RecipeDetail/RecipeRunDetail; public endpoints at config/urls.py:101-110; PublicRecipeRunPage and PublicThreadPage routed in App.tsx:21-22).

title: Tool-output rich rendering depends on a global apostrophe->double-quote replace; live stream sends empty tool input and 2000-char-truncated unparseable output
claim: ChatMessage.parseOutput handles Python-repr-shaped MCP envelopes by output.replace(/'/g,'"') before JSON.parse, so any apostrophe in data (e.g. "St. John's") breaks the parse and the rich tool card silently degrades to a raw <pre> dump (adversarial quote mixes could parse into wrong JSON). This is a frontend patch over a backend serialization leak (tool content arriving as Python repr instead of JSON). The live SSE stream compounds it: it emits tool-input-available with input:{} (the SQL/args never shown live, undercutting the prompt's mandatory provenance contract), only handles on_tool_end (no loading states), and truncates output at 2000 chars with a suffix that makes it unparseable JSON — so the rich QueryToolOutput table fails to render live for any non-trivial result; the checkpointer reload path supplies full args/untruncated output, so the same message renders differently after refresh.

votes.evidence:
I re-traced the full chain independently. The finding bundles three claims; the headline one is wrong, two are correct.

PART A — parseOutput apostrophe / "Python-repr serialization leak" (the TITLE and primary mechanism): REFUTED.
- frontend/src/components/ChatMessage/ChatMessage.tsx:24-53. parseOutput's first branch does `output.replace(/'/g,'"')` then JSON.parse and only uses the result if `Array.isArray(arr) && arr[0]?.text`. If that shape check fails it falls through to a SECOND `try { return JSON.parse(output) }`. So the apostrophe-replace only governs the array-of-text-blocks branch; it does not touch valid JSON output.
- The actual content shape is NOT a Python repr. Trace: mcp_server/server.py tools (e.g. `query` at :334) `return tc["result"]` — a plain dict envelope (mcp_server/envelope.py:34-67). FastMCP serializes a dict-returning tool into a single TextContent block whose `.text` is JSON. langchain_mcp_adapters/tools.py:88-100 builds `tool_content = [c.text for c in text_contents]` then, for the single-block case, `tool_content = tool_content[0]` — i.e. ToolMessage.content is the JSON STRING directly (double-quoted, valid JSON), with `response_format="content_and_artifact"` (tools.py:325). This is exactly what the integration test fixture encodes: tests/test_mcp_chat_integration.py:459-460 sets `content='{"success": true, "data": {...}}'` — valid double-quoted JSON, not a `[{'type':'text',...}]` repr.
- I empirically ran parseOutput against realistic shapes: a pretty/compact JSON string containing `"St. John's"` parses correctly to an object (apostrophe preserved) via the second branch; the replace-branch fails harmlessly. A hypothetical Python-repr string with an embedded apostrophe does NOT "parse into wrong JSON" — the replace makes it unparseable, both tries throw, and parseOutput returns the raw string → graceful <pre> fallback, never silent wrong-JSON. So "any apostrophe breaks the parse and the rich card silently degrades" and "adversarial quote mixes could parse into wrong JSON" are both false for what this code produces.

PART B — live SSE emits `input:{}`, only handles on_tool_end, no loading states: CONFIRMED.
- apps/chat/stream.py:191-198 emits tool-input-available with `"input": {}`. Only the `on_tool_end` branch (stream.py:159) ever fires; there is no on_tool_start/on_chat_model handling that would emit input-streaming/input-available, so ChatMessage.tsx's `isLoading` (state input-streaming/input-available, line 153) is never true during a live tool call. The checkpointer/reload path (apps/chat/message_converter.py:56-71) instead supplies `"input": tc.get("args", {})` (full SQL/args) at line 61.

PART C — 2000-char truncation makes non-trivial output render differently live vs after refresh: CONFIRMED.
- stream.py:200-203 truncates to 2000 chars and appends `\n\n... (truncated, N chars total)`, making the JSON unparseable. I verified: a >2000-char query envelope, truncated this way, makes parseOutput return a string → renderToolOutput returns null (typeof !== object) → falls to the <pre> dump (ChatMessage.tsx:298-304). The checkpointer path (message_converter.py:69) calls _tool_content_to_str with NO truncation → full JSON → parseOutput returns an object → QueryToolOutput rich table renders. So the same tool result renders as a raw <pre> live and as a rich table after refresh. The truncation behavior is locked in by tests/test_mcp_chat_integration.py:492-524.

Both live (apps/chat/views.py:239) and reload (apps/chat/thread_views.py:109) paths are wired to live routes, so the divergence is reachable. base_system.py:53,68-79 does mandate provenance, and the empty live `input:{}` does hide the SQL live — that part of the provenance argument holds.
votes.notes: Net: the live-vs-checkpointer divergence (empty input + 2000-char truncation breaking the rich render until refresh) is a real correctness/UX defect and is reachable. But the finding's headline mechanism — that tool content leaks as a Python repr and that an apostrophe-to-double-quote global replace corrupts data like "St. John's" into a degraded/wrong render — is incorrect: the content is valid double-quoted JSON, the replace lives behind a shape-gated branch, and apostrophes parse fine. The replace-branch is essentially defensive/dead code for the dict-returning MCP tools. Severity should be downgraded from a "serialization leak + adversarial quote corruption" framing to "live stream omits tool input and truncates output, so rich tool cards render as raw <pre> live and only become rich after a refresh." Status LATENT is reasonable (no data loss; cosmetic/correctness-of-display, and it self-heals on reload).

title: ConnectionsPage post-removal workspace-switch guard compares workspace ids to TenantMembership ids — can never fire
claim: After removing a connection, confirmRemove builds removedMembershipIds from chatbot membership_id values (str(TenantMembership.id)) and tests removedMembershipIds.has(activeDomainId) where activeDomainId is a Workspace id from workspaceApi.list(). The disjoint id spaces make the guard always false, so the 'switch away from a workspace whose data source was just removed' recovery never runs (and storeDomains in the closure is a stale pre-refetch snapshot). Residue of the old domains=tenant-memberships model.

votes.evidence:
I re-traced the full chain across all three files and the backend id sources.

1. confirmRemove builds the set from membership ids — `frontend/src/pages/ConnectionsPage/ConnectionsPage.tsx:146-148`: `const removedMembershipIds = new Set(connection.chatbots.map((cb) => cb.membership_id))`. The guard is `if (activeDomainId != null && removedMembershipIds.has(activeDomainId))` (line 151), and the recovery is `const next = storeDomains.find((d) => !removedMembershipIds.has(d.id)); if (next) setActiveDomain(next.id)` (lines 152-153).

2. `membership_id` is a TenantMembership PK — `apps/users/views.py:201-208` builds each chatbot dict with `"membership_id": str(tm.id)` where `tm` iterates `conn.memberships...` (TenantMembership). `apps/users/models.py:195-198`: `class TenantMembership` with `id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4)`.

3. `activeDomainId`/`storeDomains[].id` are Workspace PKs — ConnectionsPage reads `activeDomainId` and `storeDomains` from the store (lines 58-59). domainSlice.fetchDomains sets `domains = await workspaceApi.list()` and `activeDomainId = activeDomainId ?? (domains[0]?.id ?? null)` (`frontend/src/store/domainSlice.ts:37,43`). `workspaceApi.list()` calls `GET /api/workspaces/` (`frontend/src/api/workspaces.ts:109`), whose backend `WorkspaceListView.get` returns each item with `"id": str(m.workspace.id)` (`apps/workspaces/api/workspace_views.py:151`). `apps/workspaces/models.py:112`: `Workspace.id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4)`.

Because TenantMembership.id and Workspace.id are independent uuid4 PKs, `removedMembershipIds` (membership UUIDs) can never contain a Workspace UUID. Thus the guard at line 151 is always false and the recovery block at lines 152-153 is dead code.

Corroboration that activeDomainId is universally a Workspace id (never a membership id): it is passed directly to `/api/workspaces/${activeDomainId}/...` (ArtifactPanel.tsx:57,192), matched as `domains.find((d) => d.id === activeDomainId)` against workspace items (Sidebar.tsx:41, ChatRedirect.tsx:37, WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx:178), and set via `setActiveDomain(ws.id)` for a workspace ws (WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx:262). The domainSlice.ts:5 comment ("TenantMembership kept as alias") confirms the residue-of-old-model framing.

Stale-closure sub-claim is also correct: `storeDomains` (line 152) is captured from render scope; `fetchStoreDomains()` (line 150) mutates the Zustand store asynchronously but does not update the closure variable, so `storeDomains.find(...)` would read the pre-refetch snapshot — though this is moot since line 151 never passes.

Reachability: ConnectionsPage is routed at `/settings/connections` (frontend/src/router.tsx:38). The Remove button (ConnectionsPage.tsx:332) opens the confirm panel; the Confirm Remove button onClick={() => confirmRemove(conn)} (line 360) is the live entry point. The DELETE endpoint exists (apps/users/views.py:291-305) and archives memberships. So the dead guard sits in a live, user-reachable flow.
votes.notes: Core mechanism, id-space mismatch, dead-code conclusion, stale-closure note, and reachability all independently reproduced — CONFIRMED. One severity refinement (hence I'd lean PARTIAL-on-impact, but the defect itself is fully real so I keep CONFIRMED): the finding's impact "correctness" overstates user harm. This is a dead recovery-UX guard, not a data-loss/security/correctness-of-results issue. fetchStoreDomains() (line 150) still runs and refreshes the workspace list; the only consequence of the dead guard is that the user is NOT auto-switched away from a workspace whose backing connection was just removed — they may sit on a now-stale/empty active workspace until they manually switch. domainSlice only auto-picks a default when activeDomainId is null (line 43), so it won't self-heal, but nothing breaks functionally. I'd characterize practical severity as low (recovery-UX gap), closer to COSMETIC/DEBT than a correctness defect; I set corrected_impact to velocity/cleanup since it's residue of the old domains=memberships model. Note the recovery is doubly wrong: even if id spaces matched, a connection maps to memberships while the active selection is a workspace (which can be backed by multiple tenants), so membership-id-based exclusion is the wrong basis for workspace switching regardless. Status LATENT is correct — the branch is reachable but its effect never manifests.

title: Never-materialized multi-tenant workspace reports 'provisioning' forever: perpetual fake 'Loading data...' spinner
claim: _derive_schema_status returns 'provisioning' for any multi-tenant workspace whose WorkspaceViewSchema state is neither ACTIVE nor FAILED — including the no-row case (view_states.get(w.id) is None for a workspace never materialized). The frontend maps provisioning -> 'loading' and renders an animated spinner titled 'Loading data...' in the switcher and workspaces list, so a 2-tenant workspace created and never chatted in shows an infinite spinner implying active work, violating the honest-progress norm.

votes.evidence:
I independently reproduced the backend->frontend chain.

BACKEND (apps/workspaces/api/workspace_views.py):
- `_derive_schema_status` (lines 49-54): `if tenant_count > 1:` returns "available" only for ACTIVE view schema, "failed" for FAILED, and otherwise `return "provisioning"`. The `view_schema_state` argument is `view_states.get(w.id)` (line 104) where `view_states` is built from `WorkspaceViewSchema.objects.filter(workspace_id__in=...)` (lines 89-93). A workspace with no row gets `None`, falls through both ifs, and returns "provisioning". CONFIRMED.

REACHABILITY OF THE NO-ROW STATE:
- The WorkspaceViewSchema row is created ONLY inside materialization, via `get_or_create` in apps/workspaces/services/schema_manager.py:280-283 (build_workspace_view_schema). No signal/post_save creates it — `grep` for post_save/@receiver in apps/workspaces found none.
- Workspace creation (`WorkspaceListView.post`, lines 186-209) adds tenants with raw `WorkspaceTenant.objects.create()` (lines 193-194) and never defers materialization or rebuild. The CreateWorkspaceModal just calls `workspaceApi.create()` (CreateWorkspaceModal.tsx:154 -> POST /api/workspaces/).
- Materialization is triggered only by: the agent's run_materialization MCP tool, materialization_retry_view, or add/remove_workspace_tenant (workspace_service.py:28,71). So a 2-tenant workspace created and never chatted in genuinely has no WorkspaceViewSchema row indefinitely -> permanent "provisioning". CONFIRMED.
- Note: even add_workspace_tenant's `WorkspaceViewSchema.objects.filter(...).update(state=PROVISIONING)` (workspace_service.py:25) updates 0 rows when none exists, and rebuild_workspace_view_schema would FAIL (ValueError at schema_manager.py:270-274 when a tenant has no active schema), yielding "failed"->"empty" — not "provisioning". So the perpetual-provisioning state is specifically the never-touched create path, exactly as claimed.

FRONTEND (frontend/src/api/workspaces.ts):
- workspaceDataState (lines 83-85): `if (ws.schema_status === "provisioning") return "loading"`. CONFIRMED.

FRONTEND RENDER (WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx):
- DataIndicator (lines 56-89): `state === "loading"` renders `<Loader2 className="...animate-spin..."/>` with label "Loading data…" (lines 58-63, 74-75). CONFIRMED — animated spinner titled "Loading data…" in the switcher.

CORRECTION ON SURFACES: The finding claims the spinner shows "in the switcher and workspaces list". The WorkspacesPage (frontend/src/pages/WorkspacesPage/WorkspacesPage.tsx) is the /workspaces list and does NOT render DataIndicator or use schema_status at all — its WorkspaceRow (lines 49-80) only shows member_count, tenant badges, and a RoleBadge. So the spinner appears ONLY in the WorkspaceSwitcher popover (and any other component using DataIndicator/workspaceDataState), NOT in the workspaces list page. One of the two claimed surfaces is wrong.
votes.notes: Core mechanism CONFIRMED; downgraded to PARTIAL because the finding overstates the affected surfaces. The "workspaces list" (WorkspacesPage at /workspaces) does not render the data indicator at all — it shows no schema status. The infinite "Loading data…" spinner is real but confined to the WorkspaceSwitcher popover's DataIndicator (and the WorkspaceDetailPage uses schema_status separately — worth noting it would also reflect "provisioning"->loading via its own logic, though its "Loading data sources…" string at line 476 is a different, sources-list spinner unrelated to schema_status). Status LATENT and impact correctness are both accurate: it is an honest-progress / honest-indicator violation (matches Brian's documented norm that spinners must reflect real work), not a crash or data-loss bug, and it is not currently broken-for-everyone since it only manifests on workspaces created-and-never-chatted-in. The fix is trivial: either create a WorkspaceViewSchema row at workspace creation (so its state can be a real NEVER_BUILT/UNAVAILABLE), or have _derive_schema_status treat `view_schema_state is None` as "unavailable" rather than "provisioning".

title: WorkspaceDetailPage never clears a prior load error: subsequent successful loads still render the error screen
claim: The workspace fetch effect sets loading but never resets error; the render gate is `if (error || !workspace)`. After one failed load (stale/dead workspace link), navigating in-place to a valid workspace (same route element, e.g. via the topbar gear) succeeds but the page keeps showing the error until a full remount.

votes.notes: Status LATENT and impact correctness are both accurate. It is not BROKEN-NOW because it requires a specific precondition: a failed initial/prior load in the same mounted instance, followed by in-place (no-remount) navigation to a valid workspace. Most navigations into the detail page come from a different route element (e.g. chat route) and DO remount, which is why this hides as latent rather than constant. The one-line fix mirrors the sibling members effect: add `setError(null)` right after `setLoading(true)` at line 966. The cited file:line range (963-994) is accurate — it correctly spans the effect (963-977) through the render gate (994).

title: Always-on polling (jobs 3s + health 5s) with no visibility gating; each jobs poll triggers API-side janitor reconciliation (~5 DB queries)
claim: WorkspaceJobsProvider polls /jobs/active/ every 3s on every page for the active workspace and NetworkStatusContext polls /health/ every 5s, regardless of document.visibilityState or whether any job exists; each active-jobs request runs ~5 platform-DB queries and the stale-job reconciliation sweep (with potential reconcile writes) per stale job. Idle floor ~1,700 requests/hour per open tab (~165 queries/sec at 100 tabs), multiplying with tabs and users; the reconcile sweep is also the mechanism that makes the false-fail-resume race near-deterministic.

votes.evidence:
I re-traced the full chain independently.

POLLING INTERVALS & NO VISIBILITY GATING:
- frontend/src/hooks/useWorkspaceJobs.ts:4 `const POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 3000`; lines 86-88 `const interval = setInterval(() => { if (!cancelled) void fetchOnce() }, POLL_INTERVAL_MS)` with an immediate `void fetchOnce()` on mount. No `document.visibilityState`/`document.hidden`/`visibilitychange` anywhere in the hook.
- frontend/src/contexts/NetworkStatusContext.tsx:15 `const POLL_INTERVAL = 5000`; line 61 `const interval = setInterval(checkHealth, POLL_INTERVAL)`. No visibility gating; only listens for window `online`/`offline` events (lines 73-74).
- Grep for `visibilitychange|visibilityState|document.hidden` across frontend/src returns only frontend/src/pages/EmbedPage.tsx:81-88 — unrelated to either poller. So the "no visibility gating" claim holds.

REACHABILITY (always-on, every page, whole-app):
- frontend/src/router.tsx:17 `element: <AppLayout />` is the parent route; children (lines 19-44) are ALL authenticated pages (chat, artifacts, knowledge, recipes, data-dictionary, settings/connections, workspaces) rendered via `<Outlet/>`.
- frontend/src/components/AppLayout/AppLayout.tsx:51 `<WorkspaceJobsProvider workspaceId={activeDomainId}>` wraps the entire outlet => the 3s jobs poll runs on every authenticated page for the active workspace. (Gated only by `workspaceId` truthiness at useWorkspaceJobs.ts:48,78 — i.e. it stops only when no workspace is selected, which is not the normal logged-in state.)
- frontend/src/App.tsx:70 `<NetworkStatusProvider>` wraps the whole app => the 5s health poll runs unconditionally for the app's entire lifetime, with no workspace gate at all.

PER-REQUEST DB COST (active_jobs_view, apps/workspaces/api/jobs_views.py:82-162):
1) aresolve_workspace -> WorkspaceMembership.objects.select_related("workspace").aget(...) = 1 query (workspace_resolver.py:55).
2) `_fetch_active_jobs()` list comprehension (lines 101-115) = 1 query.
3) Stale-reconcile loop (lines 123-135): 0 queries when no active jobs exist (loop body skipped); when active jobs exist and are older than STALE_JOB_THRESHOLD it calls reconcile_stale_thread_job per job.
4) MaterializationRun bulk-fetch (lines 140-144) = ~1 query (runs even with empty job_ids).
5) recent_terminations query (lines 147-155) = 1 query.
=> Idle floor ~3-4 queries/poll; ~5 is accurate once jobs/reconcile are in play. The "~5 DB queries" figure is in the right ballpark.

RECONCILE SWEEP / WRITES (apps/workspaces/tasks.py:728-816, STALE_JOB_THRESHOLD=timedelta(minutes=10) at line 690):
- reconcile_stale_thread_job runs `_procrastinate_job_status` (1 query, line 715) then may issue `.aupdate(...)` writes flipping ThreadJob to FAILED (lines 754-759, 782-789), `_persist_synthetic_failure_message` (checkpointer writes), `_build_failure_summary_for_job` (extra MaterializationRun query, line 843-848), or `resume_thread_after_materialization.defer_async` (line 804). So the API-side poll genuinely performs reconcile writes — confirming this is the same code path that drives the false-fail-resume mechanism. This is shared with the worker janitor (expire_stale_thread_jobs, periodic cron */15, line 819).

HEALTH ENDPOINT: apps/workspaces/views.py:8-13 `health_check` returns static JsonResponse({"status":"ok"}) with ZERO DB queries. So the 5s health poll adds request/middleware load but not DB-query load — consistent with the finding attributing the ~165 queries/sec@100 tabs to the jobs poll alone (100/3 polls * ~4 queries ≈ 133/s).

REQUEST-RATE ARITHMETIC: jobs 3600/3=1200/hr + health 3600/5=720/hr = 1920/hr per tab. Finding says ~1,700/hr — slightly conservative/low but same order of magnitude.

All structural claims (intervals, no gating, every-page reachability, per-poll DB cost, reconcile-write mechanism) independently reproduced.
votes.notes:
Two minor numeric corrections, neither changes the verdict or severity classification:

1) "~1,700 requests/hour per open tab" is slightly low. Exact idle floor is 1200 (jobs @3s) + 720 (health @5s) = 1920 req/hr per tab. The finding undercounts.

2) The reconcile sweep is NOT part of the idle floor. It only fires for active ThreadJobs older than STALE_JOB_THRESHOLD (10 min, tasks.py:690). In steady-state idle (no jobs, or only fresh jobs) the per-poll cost is ~3-4 queries, not 5, and zero reconcile work. The finding's "~5 DB queries ... and the stale-job reconciliation sweep ... per stale job" phrasing is technically correct (it scopes the sweep "per stale job"), but a reader could mistakenly fold the sweep into the idle floor. The idle-floor DB pressure is the dominant, ever-present cost; the reconcile writes are an occasional add-on that only matters when stale jobs accumulate (e.g. the June-2026 sick-worker incident this backstop was written for).

DEBT (not BROKEN-NOW) is the correct status: the system functions correctly; this is a pure efficiency/scaling concern. The mechanism note ("reconcile sweep makes the false-fail-resume race near-deterministic") is plausible and the write path is real (tasks.py:804 defers a fresh resume for PENDING stuck jobs whose materialization finished), but I did not independently verify the race timing here — that is the subject of a separate finding. For THIS finding (polling cost), everything checks out.

reviewers cited (vertical-frontend, lens-performance-cost, extra-seam-django-state-vs-procrastinate) are consistent with what the code shows: a frontend-poll + Django-API + Procrastinate-state seam.

title: Knowledge list endpoint paginates in memory over all rows; pagination has no UI so items beyond page 1 are unreachable
claim: GET /knowledge/ loads and serializes every KnowledgeEntry and AgentLearning in the workspace on every request, merges and sorts the serialized dicts in Python, then slices the requested page (O(total) work per page view). Backend paginates at DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE=50 and the store keeps pagination metadata, but no component reads it and KnowledgeList renders no paging controls, so items beyond the first 50 are unreachable except via server-side search, and /knowledge/:id deep links only open the edit form when the item is in the loaded first page.

votes.evidence:
I independently re-traced the full chain from the live route to the consequence.

BACKEND (in-memory pagination over all rows):
- apps/knowledge/urls.py:18 wires `path("", KnowledgeListCreateView.as_view())`, included at config/urls.py:64 under `/api/workspaces/<workspace_id>/knowledge/` (urls.py docstring + view signature `def get(self, request, workspace_id)`). Live route, IsAuthenticated.
- apps/knowledge/api/views.py:23 `DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE = 50`, :24 `MAX_PAGE_SIZE = 200`.
- views.py:74-90: for each type (entry + learning), it builds `queryset = model.objects.filter(workspace=workspace)` with NO `[:n]` slice on the queryset, then `serializer = serializer_class(queryset, many=True)` and `all_items.extend(serializer.data)`. So every matching row is fetched AND serialized into Python dicts.
- views.py:92 `all_items.sort(key=lambda x: x.get("created_at", ""), reverse=True)` sorts the serialized dicts in Python.
- views.py:94-97 `total_count = len(all_items)` ... `paginated_items = all_items[start_index:end_index]` — the page slice happens only AFTER full load + serialize + sort. This is O(total) work per page view, exactly as claimed.
- views.py:101-113 returns `{"results": paginated_items, "pagination": {page, page_size, total_count, total_pages, has_next, has_previous}}`.

FRONTEND (pagination metadata stored but no consumer / no controls):
- knowledgeSlice.ts:78 declares `knowledgePagination: PaginationInfo | null`; :116 sets it from `response.pagination`. A repo-wide grep `grep -rn "knowledgePagination" src/` returns ONLY the 4 lines inside knowledgeSlice.ts itself (declaration + 3 set sites) — no component ever reads it.
- knowledgeSlice.ts:82,101-110: `fetchKnowledge` accepts `{type, search, page, pageSize}`. Grep of all callers: KnowledgePage.tsx:52 calls it with only `{type, search}` (no page); AppLayout.tsx:35 calls `fetchKnowledge()` with no args; importKnowledge re-fetch (slice:176) passes nothing. No caller ever passes `page`/`pageSize`, so only page 1 is ever requested.
- KnowledgeList.tsx (the list renderer, KnowledgePage.tsx:190): renders search + type-filter buttons + a card grid mapping `items`. There are NO prev/next/page controls anywhere in the file. Confirms items beyond page 1 are unreachable in the UI except by narrowing via server-side `search` (views.py:83-87 icontains filter).

DEEP-LINK SUB-CLAIM:
- router.tsx:31-33 defines live routes `knowledge`, `knowledge/new`, `knowledge/:id` -> KnowledgePage (also mirrored in EmbedPage.tsx:35-37).
- KnowledgePage.tsx:65-73: the `:id` effect does `const item = knowledgeItems.find((i) => i.id === id)` and opens the edit form only `if (item)`. It does NOT fetch the detail endpoint. Grep confirms there is NO `fetchKnowledgeItem`/get-by-id action in the store; the only GET to the knowledge endpoint is the list (`fetchKnowledge`). `KnowledgeDetailView` GET (views.py:175-187) exists but is never invoked from the deep-link flow. So a `/knowledge/:id` deep link for an item on page 2+ finds nothing in the loaded first page and silently fails to open the form — exactly as claimed.

Severity/impact assessment matches: DEBT / cost-perf. The O(total) serialize-then-slice is wasteful but only fires on an authenticated workspace-scoped admin page, and knowledge volumes are typically modest, so it is not BROKEN-NOW. The functional gap (unreachable items / dead deep links past page 1) is latent and only manifests once a workspace exceeds 50 combined entries+learnings.
votes.notes: All three cited file:line anchors are accurate (views.py:23,74-97; knowledgeSlice.ts:58-78; KnowledgePage.tsx:65-73). One refinement: the "items beyond the first 50 are unreachable" boundary is the COMBINED count of KnowledgeEntry + AgentLearning per workspace (page_size=50 applies to the merged list), not 50 of each. Also worth noting the in-memory sort is on the serialized `created_at` STRING (views.py:92) — fine for ISO-8601 but a latent footgun if serialization format ever changes. Reachability is solid: live React routes + live DRF endpoint + IsAuthenticated, reached whenever a user opens the Knowledge Base page (KnowledgePage.tsx:50-56 fetches on mount/domain change). No clearly-better severity downgrade warranted; CONFIRMED as DEBT/cost-perf.

title: Knowledge import 500s on malformed input / duplicate titles; export-import round trip silently loses duplicate-titled entries
claim: KnowledgeImportView catches only zipfile.BadZipFile, so parse_frontmatter's text.index ValueError on unterminated frontmatter, yaml.YAMLError, UnicodeDecodeError on non-UTF8 members, and MultipleObjectsReturned from update_or_create on (workspace,title) (KnowledgeEntry has no uniqueness constraint and create allows duplicate titles) all 500 mid-import, leaving a partial non-atomic import. On export, duplicate titles produce duplicate zip filenames; re-import keeps only the last — silent entry loss on a round trip. Export/import also covers KnowledgeEntry only; TableKnowledge annotations and AgentLearning have no portability path.

votes.evidence:
I independently re-traced the full chain from live UI to consequence; every sub-claim reproduces.

REACHABILITY (live): Routes wired in apps/knowledge/urls.py:19-20 (export/import), included at config/urls.py:64 under workspace_urlpatterns (/api/workspaces/<id>/knowledge/). Frontend buttons are live: frontend/src/pages/KnowledgePage/KnowledgePage.tsx:149 (data-testid="knowledge-export") and :157 (knowledge-import), wired to frontend/src/store/knowledgeSlice.ts:158 (getBlob .../knowledge/export/) and :174 (upload .../knowledge/import/). Both are user-reachable today.

EXCEPTION HANDLER SCOPE: apps/knowledge/api/views.py:283-313 — the try wraps the whole import loop but only `except zipfile.BadZipFile`. Verified each uncaught path raises and propagates as a 500:
- ValueError: utils.py:28 `end_idx = text.index("---", 3)`. Ran parse_frontmatter('---\ntitle: Foo\nno closing fence') -> ValueError: substring not found.
- yaml.YAMLError: utils.py:32 `yaml.safe_load`. Ran parse_frontmatter('---\ntitle: [unclosed\n---\nbody') -> yaml ParserError.
- BONUS AttributeError (not in finding): non-dict frontmatter ('---\njust a scalar\n---\nbody') -> meta.get on a str -> AttributeError. Strengthens the finding.
- UnicodeDecodeError: views.py:290 `zf.read(name).decode("utf-8")`. Verified bytes([0xff,0xfe,...]).decode('utf-8') -> UnicodeDecodeError.
- MultipleObjectsReturned: views.py:295 update_or_create(workspace, title). Confirmed via inspect that QuerySet.update_or_create -> get_or_create -> self.get(); get_or_create only catches model.DoesNotExist; QuerySet.get raises MultipleObjectsReturned. So >1 pre-existing (workspace,title) row 500s.

NO UNIQUENESS CONSTRAINT: apps/knowledge/models.py:84-115 KnowledgeEntry.Meta has only ordering + verbose_name_plural; no unique_together/UniqueConstraint on (workspace,title). The only unique_together in migrations (0002_initial.py:81) is ("workspace","table_name") — that's TableKnowledge, a different model. Serializer (apps/knowledge/api/serializers.py:8-32) has no title UniqueValidator/validate_title. POST create (views.py:115-153) just serializer.save(workspace=...) — duplicate titles are freely creatable, so MultipleObjectsReturned is reachable.

NON-ATOMIC: grep for transaction/atomic in views.py returns nothing; the loop writes entry-by-entry, so a mid-loop exception leaves a partial import.

ROUND-TRIP SILENT LOSS: Export (views.py:244-251) does zf.writestr(filename,...) per entry where filename = sanitized title (:80 chars). Reproduced that a zip with two 'Same_Title.md' members yields namelist of length 2 but read() returns only the second ('second'); last-wins. Additionally the sanitizer (views.py:246-248) collapses distinct titles (e.g. 'Foo/Bar' and 'Foo_Bar') into the same filename, broadening loss beyond exact-duplicate titles. On re-import, update_or_create on title keeps only the last -> silent entry loss.

PORTABILITY SCOPE: export/import (views.py:227-318) reference KnowledgeEntry only. AgentLearning appears only in list/create config (views.py:33-34) and is blocked from manual create (:124); TableKnowledge is not referenced in views.py at all. No export/import path for either.

LATENT confirmed: no test exercises these paths — grep of apps/knowledge/tests + tests/ for KnowledgeImportView/KnowledgeExportView/parse_frontmatter round-trips found no coverage of the import/export views or malformed-input handling.
votes.notes: All sub-claims hold and reachability is current (live UI + routes). Status LATENT is correct: real defects, no triggering test, but trivially triggered by any user uploading a malformed/non-UTF8 zip, by exporting+re-importing when duplicate titles exist, or by importing into a workspace that already has duplicate (workspace,title) rows. Two strengthening additions the finding omitted: (1) a non-dict YAML frontmatter also 500s via AttributeError on meta.get; (2) the export filename sanitizer can collapse DISTINCT titles into one filename, so silent round-trip loss is not limited to literal duplicate titles. Impact 'correctness' (silent data loss on a knowledge round trip + 500s) is apt; I'd note it edges toward data-loss for the round-trip case, but correctness is defensible since the source rows still exist in the original workspace.

title: Learning lifecycle is inert: confidence never auto-adjusts, times_applied effectively never increments, but the prompt implies real usage
claim: The advertised feedback loop doesn't exist: increase/decrease_confidence are callable only from Django admin bulk actions (the model help_text claims auto-adjust on confirm/contradict); times_applied increments only when the agent re-saves a byte-identical (iexact) description — essentially never for LLM prose — yet the retriever renders '(applied N times)' implying real usage. Drifted learnings never retire. save_learning's only validation compares applies_to_tables against the dead Workspace.data_dictionary field (always empty), so it is permanently skipped.

votes.evidence:
I independently re-traced the full chain. All four sub-claims hold.

(1) increase/decrease_confidence are admin-only. Model methods at apps/knowledge/models.py:192-202. The ONLY callers are apps/knowledge/admin.py:165 and :173 (the "Increase/Decrease confidence (+/-10%)" bulk actions). grep across apps/, config/, mcp_server/ returns no other callers. There is NO confirm/contradict auto-adjust: grep -niE "contradict|confirm|feedback" over apps/knowledge, apps/agents, apps/chat returns ONLY the help_text string itself (models.py:169) and its migration. The help_text "Increases when the learning is confirmed useful, decreases if contradicted" is unbacked by any code path.

(2) times_applied is effectively inert. The only increment is apps/agents/tools/learning_tool.py:191 (`existing.times_applied += 1`), reached ONLY when a prior active learning matches `description__iexact=description.strip()` (learning_tool.py:182-186) — a case-insensitive byte-identical match on the full description. New learnings are created with times_applied=0 (line 219) and there is no other write site (grep confirms only models, admin, serializers, retriever ordering reference it). So the "applied" counter never increments on normal use; it would require the LLM to re-emit an identical description string.

(3) The retriever renders "(applied N times)" implying real usage. Located at apps/knowledge/services/retriever.py:137 (the finding's cited line 33 is wrong — that's the retrieve() signature; this is a line-number imprecision, not a behavior error). The render is gated on `confidence_score >= 0.8` (line 134). This output IS live: _build_system_prompt calls KnowledgeRetriever(workspace).retrieve() at apps/agents/graph/base.py:730-731 and injects it into the system prompt (line 733). The save_learning tool is also live: _build_tools always appends create_save_learning_tool(workspace, user) at base.py:693.

(4) Table validation is permanently skipped. learning_tool.py:168 reads `workspace.data_dictionary or {}`. The Workspace.data_dictionary JSONField (apps/workspaces/models.py:132) is NEVER written anywhere — grep for assignments found zero writes; the only reads are this validation, purge_synced_data.py (which only clears it), and apps/workspaces/api/views.py:425-426 explicitly labeled "Fallback: legacy data_dictionary JSONField". So known_tables (line 169) is always empty, making the `if known_tables:` block (lines 171-179) dead. Even if reached, it only logs a warning and continues (line 179 comment: "Don't fail"), so it is non-validating by design.

(5) No drift/retirement/decay: the only is_active=False write is the manual admin reject_learnings action (admin.py:158). Nothing automatically retires or decays drifted learnings.

Reachability: CONFIRMED live — save_learning is an always-on agent tool, the retriever feeds the live chat system prompt, and the admin actions are reachable via Django admin. The defect is that the advertised self-improving feedback loop is non-functional, not that the path is dead.
votes.notes: Verdict CONFIRMED. Two minor imprecisions in the finding, neither affecting the substance: (a) the "(applied N times)" render is at retriever.py:137, not :33 (line 33 is the retrieve() method signature); (b) the finding omits that the "applied N times" line only renders when confidence_score >= 0.8 (retriever.py:134) — but since duplicate re-saves bump confidence by +0.1 from a 0.5 start, a learning reaching 0.8 has by construction matched iexact at least 3 times, so a nonzero count would display, which actually reinforces the "implies real usage" point rather than weakening it. Status DEBT and impact correctness are both appropriate: the lifecycle machinery exists and is wired (not LATENT/dead), but the advertised auto-adjusting/retiring feedback loop does not function, and the prompt asserts usage metrics that are essentially always meaningless. Impact leans toward correctness because false "applied N times / confidence X%" signals are injected into the LLM's system prompt and can miscalibrate the model's trust in learnings; one could argue COSMETIC for the prompt-text aspect, but the inert lifecycle (no decay/retirement of drifted learnings) is a genuine correctness gap, so correctness is the better classification.
